name: org.machanism.machai:genai-client
description:
GenAI Client is a Java library in the MachAI/Machanism ecosystem that provides a provider-neutral API for interacting with generative AI / LLM backends, including prompt orchestration, optional file attachments, embedding generation, token-usage reporting, and host-integrated tool/function calling.

Core contract: org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenAIProvider defines a session-like interface: init(org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator), instructions(java.lang.String), prompt(java.lang.String), addFile(java.io.File) / addFile(java.net.URL) to attach inputs, addTool(java.lang.String, java.lang.String, GenAIProvider.ToolFunction, java.lang.String...) to register host-side functions, perform() to execute, embedding(java.lang.String, long) to generate vectors, clear() to reset conversation state, inputsLog(java.io.File) to log inputs, setWorkingDir(java.io.File) to scope file/tool operations, and usage() returning org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.Usage (inputTokens, inputCachedTokens, outputTokens).

Provider resolution: org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenAIProviderManager.getProvider(java.lang.String, org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator) resolves a provider from a Provider:Model identifier (e.g., "OpenAI:gpt-4o-mini" or "CodeMie:gpt-4o-mini"). If the provider prefix is omitted, the default provider is org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.none.NoneProvider (NAME = "None"). Resolution supports short provider names mapped to org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.<provider-lowercase>.<Provider>Provider or fully-qualified class names. The manager sets the resolved model into the provided configurator as key "chatModel" before calling provider.init(conf). Token usage metrics can be aggregated and logged by the manager.

Included providers and behaviors:
- org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.openai.OpenAIProvider: integrates the OpenAI Java SDK Responses API; supports multi-turn prompt accumulation, instructions, local/URL file inputs, function-tool calling with iterative tool-call resolution, and embeddings. Configuration keys read from Configurator: chatModel (required; model passed to OpenAI Responses API), OPENAI_API_KEY (required), OPENAI_BASE_URL (optional for OpenAI-compatible endpoints), GENAI_TIMEOUT (seconds, optional; if <=0 SDK defaults are used), MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS (optional; defaults to 300000), MAX_TOOL_CALLS (optional; defaults to 200). Tool call handlers receive Object[2] where [0] is com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.JsonNode arguments and [1] is the working directory java.io.File.
- org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.codemie.CodeMieProvider: integrates EPAM CodeMie by requesting an OAuth2/OIDC token from AUTH_URL (default CodeMie token endpoint) using GENAI_USERNAME/GENAI_PASSWORD. It sets OPENAI_BASE_URL to the CodeMie API base URL and OPENAI_API_KEY to the token, then delegates based on chatModel prefix: gpt-* delegates to OpenAIProvider; gemini-* delegates to GeminiProvider; claude-* delegates to ClaudeProvider. It rejects configurations where the OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable is set to avoid conflicts.
- org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.none.NoneProvider: no-op/offline provider for restricted environments; accumulates prompts, optionally writes prompts to inputsLog and instructions to instructions.txt, perform() returns null, embeddings unsupported.
- org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.gemini.GeminiProvider and org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.claude.ClaudeProvider: currently placeholders/scaffolds; most operations are not implemented.

Host-integrated tools (function calling): the SPI org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionTools allows curated tool installers to register named tools on a provider. org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionToolsLoader discovers implementations via java.util.ServiceLoader and applies them; it can propagate a Configurator to tool installers for runtime placeholder resolution in tool arguments (for example ${...} inside headers/URLs/commands).
- FileFunctionTools: read_file_from_file_system, write_file_to_file_system, list_files_in_directory, get_recursive_file_list; all paths are interpreted relative to provider working directory.
- CommandFunctionTools: run_command_line_tool and terminate_process; enforces deny-list checks (OS-specific resources denylist/unix.txt and denylist/windows.txt), confines the working directory to within the project root, and bounds captured output via LimitedStringBuilder.
- WebFunctionTools: get_web_content (HTTP GET with optional textOnly rendering and optional CSS selector extraction via Jsoup) and call_rest_api (generic REST call). Supports optional Basic Auth via URL userInfo and resolves ${...} placeholders in headers/URLs using Configurator.

Typical use cases: backend services and automation tooling needing unified access to multiple LLM vendors/endpoints, prompt execution/orchestration, safe host-side tool invocation during tool-calling, embedding pipelines for semantic search/RAG, and consolidated token usage accounting.

Key dependencies: org.machanism.macha.core.commons:configurator, com.openai:openai-java, org.jsoup:jsoup, net.htmlparser.jericho:jericho-html, org.apache.commons:commons-text, org.apache.maven.shared:maven-shared-utils, com.google.guava:guava, commons-lang:commons-lang, commons-io:commons-io, org.slf4j:slf4j-api, com.anthropic:anthropic-java, com.google.genai:google-genai.

usageContext: Tool/function calling with controlled host capabilities

constructors.description: Creates a delegating GenAIProvider adapter without an initial delegate. Subclasses should call setProvider(org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenAIProvider) before any other operation; otherwise calls will fail due to a missing delegate.

examples.snippet: org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenAIAdapter adapter = new org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenAIAdapter();
examples.description: Creates an adapter instance; typically subclassed to set the delegate provider during initialization.

constructors.description: Creates an OpenAI-backed provider instance. Call init(org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator) to configure chatModel, credentials, and optional limits/timeouts before use.

examples.description: Creates and initializes the provider. The configurator must supply OPENAI_API_KEY and chatModel; OPENAI_BASE_URL, GENAI_TIMEOUT, MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS, and MAX_TOOL_CALLS are optional.

constructors.description: Creates a CodeMie provider instance that authenticates against an OIDC token endpoint and then delegates to OpenAIProvider/GeminiProvider/ClaudeProvider depending on the chatModel prefix.

examples.description: Instantiates and initializes the provider. The configurator must supply GENAI_USERNAME, GENAI_PASSWORD, and chatModel (AUTH_URL optional).

constructors.description: Creates a no-op provider suitable for disabled/offline environments. It can accumulate prompts and optionally log instructions/prompts to disk via inputsLog(java.io.File).

examples.description: Uses NoneProvider to log inputs and returns null as no model is invoked.

constructors.description: Creates an immutable usage record representing token counts for a single invocation. Providers can return this from usage(), and GenAIProviderManager can aggregate it.

examples.snippet: org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.Usage usage = new org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.Usage(100L, 0L, 250L);
examples.description: Creates a Usage instance with explicit input, cached input, and output token counts.

features.name: getProvider
features.description: Resolves and instantiates a GenAIProvider implementation based on a provider/model identifier formatted as Provider:Model. If the provider prefix is omitted, the default None provider is used. Providers can be referenced via short names (mapped to org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.<provider>.<Provider>Provider) or by fully-qualified class name. The method sets the resolved model name into the configurator under key "chatModel" and invokes provider.init(conf).

examples.description: Resolves an OpenAI provider instance for model gpt-4o-mini and runs a simple prompt.

features.name: prompt
features.description: Adds a user prompt to the current provider session/conversation. Providers may accumulate multiple prompts before execution via perform().

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenAIProvider provider = null;
provider.prompt("Summarize the following text...");
examples.description: Adds a prompt to the provider's current session.

features.name: instructions
features.description: Sets system/session instructions that guide model behavior for the current conversation (for example role, constraints, or tone).

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenAIProvider provider = null;
provider.instructions("You are a concise assistant.");
examples.description: Sets system instructions used by the provider for subsequent execution.

features.name: addFile
features.description: Attaches a local file resource for provider-side processing. Concrete providers may upload the file (e.g., OpenAIProvider uploads to OpenAI Files API and attaches by file_id) or ignore it (NoneProvider).

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenAIProvider provider = null;
provider.addFile(new java.io.File("./docs/spec.pdf"));
examples.description: Attaches a local file to the current request context.

features.name: addTool
features.signature: void org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenAIProvider.addTool(java.lang.String name, java.lang.String description, org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenAIProvider.ToolFunction function, java.lang.String... paramsDesc)
features.description: Registers a host-side tool (function) that the provider may invoke during a model run. In OpenAIProvider, handlers receive Object[2] where index 0 is a parsed com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.JsonNode of arguments and index 1 is the working directory provided via setWorkingDir(java.io.File).

examples.description: Registers a simple tool named "echo" that returns a JSON argument value (OpenAIProvider argument convention).

features.name: perform
features.description: Executes the provider using accumulated prompts, instructions, attachments, and registered tools, returning response text when available. Providers may support iterative tool-call resolution (e.g., OpenAIProvider) and may return null for no-op providers (NoneProvider).

examples.description: Runs a prompt execution and reads the response.

features.name: embedding
features.description: Computes an embedding vector for input text when supported by the provider (e.g., OpenAIProvider). Providers that do not support embeddings may throw UnsupportedOperationException (e.g., NoneProvider).

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenAIProvider provider = null;
java.util.List<java.lang.Double> vector = provider.embedding("hello", 1536L);
examples.description: Requests an embedding vector of a given dimensionality.

features.name: applyTools
features.description: Discovers FunctionTools implementations via ServiceLoader and applies them to a provider by registering their tools (file tools, command tools, web tools) using GenAIProvider.addTool(...).

examples.description: Configures and installs host-side tools into a provider using the singleton loader, then sets the working directory used by tools.

customizations.name: FunctionTools
customizations.description: Service-provider interface (SPI) used to contribute host-side tools (functions) that can be registered with a GenAIProvider. Implementations are commonly discovered via java.util.ServiceLoader and applied by FunctionToolsLoader. Implementations may also resolve ${...} placeholders using a Configurator.

examples.description: Implements the FunctionTools SPI to register a custom tool with a provider.

customizations.name: GenAIAdapter
customizations.description: Delegating GenAIProvider adapter intended for decoration. Subclasses can intercept calls (for example, prompt, perform, addTool) while delegating to an underlying provider configured via setProvider(GenAIProvider).

examples.description: Demonstrates decorating a provider to add logging while preserving the GenAIProvider contract.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenAIProvider
studs.description: Provider contract for generative-AI integrations, including prompt/instructions accumulation, optional file inputs, tool registration, execution, usage reporting, and lifecycle operations.

examples.description: Uses the GenAIProvider interface to interact with a concrete provider resolved at runtime.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionTools
studs.description: SPI for installing host-provided tools into a GenAIProvider instance.

examples.description: Applies a specific FunctionTools implementation directly to a provider.

examples.title: Provider resolution and basic prompt execution
Installing host-side tools via ServiceLoader
Using the no-op provider for input logging only
